video:video.video:title: Severe flooding hits Russia’s Dagestan region
video:video.video:description: Severe flooding hit Russia’s southern Republic of Dagestan on Saturday following heavy rainfall. A railway bridge collapsed, and power was cut off in 132 settlements across Dagestan.

video:video.video:title: Inside the lab where NASA’s moon mission spacesuits are made
video:video.video:description: NASA is getting ready to send astronauts on a 10-day trip around the moon. The spacesuits they’ll wear are designed for the Orion capsule and future missions to Mars.

video:video.video:title: Inside the lab where NASA’s moon mission spacesuits are made
video:video.video:description: NASA astronauts are gearing up to fly around the moon for the first time in more than a half-century.

video:video.video:title: Iran-backed Houthis enter the monthlong war and could further threaten global shipping - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels entered the month-long war in the Middle East on Saturday, claiming two missile launches at Israel.

video:video.video:title: Black smoke rises from industrial site in southern Israel hit by fragment of intercepted missile
video:video.video:description: Source: ISRAELI FIRE AND RESCUE AUTHORITY Heavy black smoke was seen rising Sunday at an industrial plant in southern Israel after what Israeli authorities said was an "interception fragment impact” from an Iranian missile

video:video.video:title: Missing sailboats carrying aid land in Cuba after being located by Mexican navy
video:video.video:description: Two sailboats that went missing carrying humanitarian aid from southern Mexico to Cuba landed in Havana on Saturday afternoon hours after Mexico's navy said it had located the boats days after they went incommunicado because of bad weather.

video:video.video:title: Funerals held in Khan Younis, Gaza for Palestinians including 3 police killed in Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: Two Israeli strikes early Sunday killed six Palestinians, including three policemen, in the Gaza Strip, hospital authorities said.

video:video.video:title: AP reports: Iranians at border crossing into Iraq urge US to end the war
video:video.video:description: Iranians crossing into southern Iraq on Sunday urged the United States to end the war, describing relentless airstrikes, rising prices and worsening living conditions, but insisting they would not leave their country.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather at Chile harvest festival, celebrating wine and tradition
video:video.video:description: Thousands of visitors attended the Panquehue Harvest Festival in Chile’s Valparaíso Region on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Faithful celebrate Palm Sunday in Ecuador and Peru
video:video.video:description: Faithful in Ecuador and Peru celebrate Palm Sunday, marking the official start of Holy Week. (AP video shot by: Mauricio Muñoz)

video:video.video:title: Egypt introduces 9 p.m. closing time for commercial premises in bid to save electricity
video:video.video:description: As Egypt seeks ways to conserve oil-powered electricity, one decision appears to hit at the core identity of Cairo as a city that prides itself on never sleeping.

video:video.video:title: Bird sellers carry towers of caged songbirds in annual Palm Sunday pilgrimage to receive blessings
video:video.video:description: For 32 years, Cruz Monroy has wandered the streets of a small town on the fringes of Mexico's capital with a tower of small cages filled with a rainbow of birds from all over the world.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian Catholics attend Palm Sunday Mass in Gaza City
video:video.video:description: Christians gathered at the Holy Family Church in Gaza to celebrate Palm Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian inmates reduce sentences through reading
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of Brazil's detainees are part of a programme where reading books reduces their sentences. (AP/ Lucas Dumphreys)

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo prays for Christians in the Middle East at the Vatican's Palm Sunday Mass
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV on Sunday rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St.

video:video.video:title: Pakistani foreign minister says his country will host talks between US and Iran
video:video.video:description: "Pakistan will be honored to host and facilitate meaningful talks between the two sides in the coming days,” Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar said in a speech after top diplomats from Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia met in Islamabad.

video:video.video:title: Actor Antonio Banderas leads Palm Sunday procession in his hometown of Malaga
video:video.video:description: Actor Antonio Banderas joined the crowds in a Palm Sunday procession on Sunday, marking the official start of Holy Week in Spain, a country with a fervent Catholic tradition.

video:video.video:title: Chesney the kangaroo scales tall fence and flees petting zoo in Wisconsin for three days
video:video.video:description: Chesney the kangaroo jumped an eight-foot fence to escape his petting zoo in Wisconsin, spending three days on the lam.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese on streets of Beirut condemn 'expansionist' Israel as it seeks to widen invasion
video:video.video:description: People in Lebanon displaced by the ongoing fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group condemned Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu's announcement he was widening Israel's invasion of the south of the country.

video:video.video:title: Iran University of Science and Technology building reduced to rubble by Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: The country's Revolutionary Guard warned that Iran would consider Israeli universities and branches of U.S. universities in the region “legitimate targets” unless offered safety assurances for Iranian universities, state media reported.

video:video.video:title: Semenya criticizes IOC chief Coventry after policy shift on women's Olympic events
video:video.video:description: Two-time Olympic champion runner Caster Semenya on Sunday expressed her disappointment with International Olympic Committee President Kirsty Coventry over the decision to ban transgender athletes from competing in women's events at the Olympics.

video:video.video:title: Kosovo, one of Europe's poorest countries, struggles as Iran war drives up fuel prices
video:video.video:description: Kushtrim Ajvazi is proud to be part of a successful business in one of the poorest countries in Europe.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 29
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, March 29: Pakistan's foreign minister says they will soon host talks betwen the U.S. and Iran; PM Netanyahu says Israel will widen its invasion of southern Lebanon; severe weather in Afghanistan kills at least 17 people; Jerusalem holy sites closed ahead of Passover and

video:video.video:title: Humpback whale freed by rescuers in Baltic Sea has become stranded again
video:video.video:description: A humpback whale that was freed after becoming stuck for several days in shallow water at a Baltic Sea resort in Germany was stranded again on Saturday after failing to find its way back to the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 30
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday March 30th: Trump says U.S. and Iran negotiating directly and indirectly; Iran strikes water and electrical plant in Kuwait; U.S. airports urge passengers to arrive early due to long lines; Mission to moon set to blast off this week.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong and Asian markets down as oil price worries dominate
video:video.video:description: Stock markets in Asia slumped in Monday morning trading, as worries continued about soaring oil prices and the risk of US escalation in the war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Fire at oil refinery in Israel's Haifa for second time during Iran war
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out Monday at an oil refinery in the northern Israeli city of Haifa for the second time during the monthlong war with Iran, according to footage from the scene.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya's something old, new, borrowed and....
video:video.video:description: Is Zendaya embracing her bridal era during "The Drama" press tour? On theme with the movie's wedding storyline, the actor seems to be bringing the tradition of wearing something old, something new, something borrowed and something blue to red carpets worldwide.

video:video.video:title: 'Decisive day' for humpback whale stranded off German coast amid fading hopes it can regain strength
video:video.video:description: A humpback whale stranded off Germany's north coast faces a critical moment in its hopes for survival, experts said Monday.

video:video.video:title: The Supreme Court will decide whether Trump’s birthright citizenship order violates the Constitution
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court will hear arguments on Wednesday over the constitutionality of President Donald Trump’s order on birthright citizenship.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Apollo to Artemis: A look at the legacy of NASA’s moon missions and what’s next
video:video.video:description: NASA is going back to the moon more than a half-century after Apollo. NASA’s Artemis program is a follow-up to the Apollo moonshots of the late 1960s and early 1970s.

video:video.video:title: Cuba prepares to receive first oil shipment this year after ease in US blockade
video:video.video:description: Cuba prepared on Monday to receive a sanctioned Russian tanker carrying roughly 730,000 barrels of oil, marking the island’s first such delivery this year.

video:video.video:title: The Supreme Court to hear arguments this week over Trump’s birthright citizenship order
video:video.video:description: Justices could decide this summer the constitutionality of Trump’s order. The 14th Amendment and federal law are widely understood to make citizens of everyone born in the country, with narrow exceptions.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Jews worldwide celebrate Passover
video:video.video:description: Passover is a major Jewish holiday, celebrated over the course of a week each year in springtime. It commemorates the exodus of ancient Israelites from slavery in Egypt, as recounted in the Bible.

video:video.video:title: Sky turned red ahead of tropical cyclone in Western Australia
video:video.video:description: The sky turned blood red in Western Australia’s Shark Bay ahead of the arrival of Tropical Cyclone Narelle on Friday.

video:video.video:title: 'We are still living in a war': Palestinian mourns brother among three men killed by Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Mourners prayed outside the Shifa Hospital in northern Gaza on Monday for three members of Hamas' military wing who were killed in an Israeli airstrike.

video:video.video:title: The giant Easter egg that weighs 55kg
video:video.video:description: British chocolate maker Cadbury has unveiled a giant 55 kilogram (121.25 pounds) egg ahead of Easter.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: Trump says US could move to seize control of Iran's Kharg oil export hub
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump has suggested American troops could seize Iran's Kharg Island oil export hub, even as he added in comments published Monday that a deal with Tehran to end the war “could be made fairly quickly.”

video:video.video:title: Apollo to Artemis: A look at the legacy of NASA’s moon missions and what’s next
video:video.video:description: NASA is going back to the moon more than a half-century after Apollo. The Artemis mission with four astronauts on board won't land on the moon this time or even orbit it.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday night said he has “no problem” with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian inmates reduce sentences through reading
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of detainees across Brazil — including former President Jair Bolsonaro — signed up for a sentence reduction program that encourages inmates to immerse themselves in literary works in exchange for reducing their sentences by up to 48 days per year.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike targets apartment building in Beirut’s southern suburbs
video:video.video:description: Smoke billowed Monday from an apartment targeted by an Israeli airstrike in the southern suburbs of Beirut.

video:video.video:title: Black smoke cloud rises from fire at factory hit by missile fragment in southern Israel
video:video.video:description: A missile fragment that fell on a factory in southern Israel set a pesticide tank on fire sending black plumes of smoke and billowing flames high into the air.

video:video.video:title: Buses and trucks conduct slow-moving protest against rising fuel prices in Paris
video:video.video:description: Dozens of buses and trucks conducted a slow-moving protest on Paris' Boulevard Périphérique on Monday against the rise in fuel prices due to the war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Taiwan fashion designer stuns audience with avant-garde costumes
video:video.video:description: A fashion designer has taken inspiration from historic museums for his latest collection. On the catwalk at Taipei Fashion Week, Justin Chou's clothes include wigs, jackets and crowns drawn from paintings of the European nobles of the past.

video:video.video:title: US stocks fall in another shaky day as oil prices keep climbing
video:video.video:description: After jumping to an initial gain of 0.9%, the S&P 500 quickly erased nearly all of it before seesawing lower.

video:video.video:title: Iraqi military aircraft destroyed in missile attack on Baghdad air base
video:video.video:description: A rocket attack targeted an Iraqi air base west of Baghdad's airport destroyed a military aircraft, a spokesperson for the country's defense ministry said Monday.

video:video.video:title: Fed Chair Powell talks about supply shocks from Iran war in appearance at Harvard
video:video.video:description: Fed Chair Jerome Powell discussed energy price spikes caused by the Iran war and other issues during a talk Monday at Harvard University

video:video.video:title: Former Pakistani ambassador analyzes prospects for ending Iran war as his country seeks to mediate
video:video.video:description: Pakistan’s former ambassador to Iran said prospects for renewed U.S.-Iran dialogue may depend on an initial ceasefire and mutual willingness to ease hostilities, while warning that continued conflict could have far-reaching global consequences.

video:video.video:title: Mexico weighs oil cooperation with Cuba as Trump signals openness to Russian shipment
video:video.video:description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday her government is exploring both humanitarian aid and potential commercial fuel arrangements with Cuba, as shifting U.S. signals and a pending Russian oil delivery draw renewed attention to the island’s energy crisis.

video:video.video:title: White House says the Iranian officials ‘are appearing more reasonable behind the scenes’ in talks
video:video.video:description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt says Iranian officials are appearing "more reasonable" than former regime leaders, "who are now no longer on planet Earth."

video:video.video:title: Trump threatens to destroy Iran’s civilian infrastructure if a deal is not reached ‘shortly’
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Monday threatened widespread destruction of Iran’s energy resources and other vital infrastructure, including desalination plants, if a deal to end the war is not reached “shortly.”

video:video.video:title: Leavitt insists US military will act within law as Trump threatens Iran's energy sites
video:video.video:description: The White House said Monday talks with Iran are going well, but warned President Donald Trump is "not afraid" to carry out threats against Iranian energy sites if they fail.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 30
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, March 30th: White House insists no change in Cuba sanction policy; Israel's parliament approves death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis; NASA prepares for moon rocket launch; Peruvian activist and her piglet bring attention to animal rights

video:video.video:title: MLB introduces new replay hand signal: two pats on the head
video:video.video:description: Major League Baseball has a new entry in the lexicon of sports replay hand signals: A couple pats on the head.

video:video.video:title: Israel's parliament approves the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis
video:video.video:description: Israel’s parliament on Monday passed a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis, a measure that has been harshly condemned by the international community and rights groups as discriminatory and inhumane.

video:video.video:title: Replay hand signals are everywhere in sports
video:video.video:description: Major League Baseball has a new entry in the lexicon of sports replay hand signals: A couple pats on the head.

video:video.video:title: Cuba prepares to receive first oil shipment this year
video:video.video:description: Cuba prepared Monday to receive a sanctioned Russian tanker carrying roughly 730,000 barrels of oil, the first such fuel delivery this year to the island that has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

video:video.video:title: A 'World Cup' for immigrant girls uses the joy of sport to counter ICE fears
video:video.video:description: A community advocate and immigrant from Bhutan created the event to help provide a sense of joy and unity amid federal immigration enforcement operations that have affected players' families.

video:video.video:title: TSA workers get paid and some lines improve — but ICE isn’t leaving yet
video:video.video:description: TSA workers are getting paid again and some lines are improving — but ICE officers are still at airports.

video:video.video:title: Celebs support as Brandy gets star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Brandy was joined at her Hollywood Walk of Fame ceremony Monday by Babyface, Issa Rae, Monica, Kehlani and "Moesha" co-stars Shar Jackson and Marcus T. Paulk.

video:video.video:title: US senators visit Taiwan to reinforce security and economic ties
video:video.video:description: A bipartisan group of four U.S. senators arrived in Taiwan early Monday for a two-day visit aimed at strengthening alliances in the Indo-Pacific and reaffirming Washington’s support for the island.

video:video.video:title: Israel's parliament passes budget in marathon overnight session
video:video.video:description: Israel's parliament passes budget in marathon overnight session

video:video.video:title: Brandy hopes her journey teaches authenticity
video:video.video:description: Here's what Brandy hopes her journey teaches young women. She was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame.

video:video.video:title: Central African Republic's President Faustin-Archange Touadéra sworn in for third term
video:video.video:description: The president of the Central African Republic, Faustin-Archange Touadéra, was sworn in for a third term on Monday three months after a disputed general election.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian toddlers evacuated in 2023 as premature babies return to families in a transformed Gaza
video:video.video:description: A group of toddlers evacuated as premature infants just a month into the Israel-Hamas war returned Monday to families overjoyed yet worried about their futures in a transformed Gaza.

video:video.video:title: U.S. Central Command releases video of military strikes in Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Central Command on Monday posted on the social media platform X video of military strikes in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Israel's parliament approves the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of killing Israelis
video:video.video:description: Israel’s parliament on Monday passed a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis, a measure that has been harshly condemned by the international community and rights groups as discriminatory and inhumane.

video:video.video:title: Jonathan Tropper: Ryan Gosling brings 'charisma' to Star Wars
video:video.video:description: Ryan Gosling leads the latest film in the "Star Wars" franchise, which is scheduled to hit theaters in 2027. (March 30)

video:video.video:title: Auschwitz survivor Ginette Kolinka reflects on Nazi horrors at 101
video:video.video:description: A 101-year-old survivor of Auschwitz has, with help from Steven Spielberg, become a mighty warrior against antisemitism in France.

video:video.video:title: UN says 3 peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon in 24 hours
video:video.video:description: The United Nations’ most powerful body will convene an emergency session Tuesday after officials announced that three peacekeepers in southern Lebanon had been killed in the last 24 hours.

video:video.video:title: Olivia Munn on Savannah Guthrie: 'She doesn't deserve this'
video:video.video:description: Munn has co-hosted the "Today" show and says she is heartbroken for Guthrie, whose mother remaining missing. (March 30)

video:video.video:title: US Central Command releases video of military strikes in Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Central Command on Monday posted on the social media platform X video of military strikes in Iran.

video:video.video:title: The revival efforts for traditional Punjabi Phulkari embroidery
video:video.video:description: The traditional “flower work” embroidery from India’s northern Punjab region is being reimagined by artisans to preserve its original techniques.
